records.ety: [L. terrere to frighten + -fy: cf. F. terrifier, L. terrificare. See Terrific, and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Terrified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Terrifying (?).]

quotes: If the law, instead of aggravating and terrifying sin, shall give out license, it foils itself.

defs: To alarm or shock with fear; to frighten.
quotes: When ye shall hear of wars . . . be not terrified.
